title: Peking Duck

seo.title: Peking Duck Recipe: Crispy Skin & Authentic Flavor
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of Peking Duck with our authentic recipe. Achieve perfectly crispy skin and tender meat at home with step-by-step instructions. A Chinese feast!
seo.h1: Authentic Peking Duck Recipe: Crispy Skin at Home
h2Headings: Pro Tips for the Crispiest Skin
seo.imageAlt: A whole roasted Peking Duck with crispy skin, served with thin pancakes, scallions, and hoisin sauce.
seo.imageCaption: Our authentic Peking Duck recipe promises a crispy, flavorful experience perfect for a special occasion.

description: Peking Duck is a legendary Chinese dish celebrated for its incredibly crispy skin and succulent, tender meat. This elaborate preparation involves a series of steps to achieve its iconic texture and rich flavor. Served traditionally with thin pancakes, scallions, and hoisin sauce, it is a true culinary masterpiece.

ingredients.notes: head and feet removed, thoroughly cleaned

instructions.title: Prepare and Blanch the Duck
instructions.text: Thoroughly clean the duck, ensuring no pin feathers remain. Pat it completely dry inside and out. Carefully separate the skin from the meat around the neck and breast using your fingers, being careful not to tear the skin. Bring a large pot of water to a rolling boil. Carefully pour boiling water over the duck's skin, ensuring all parts are blanched. This helps tighten the skin.

instructions.title: Air Dry for Crispy Skin
instructions.text: Immediately hang the blanched duck in a cool, well-ventilated area or in front of a fan for at least 12-24 hours, or until the skin is completely dry and taut. This crucial step is essential for achieving the signature crispy skin.

instructions.title: Apply the Glaze
instructions.text: In a bowl, mix the dissolved maltose (or honey), rice vinegar, Shaoxing wine, five-spice powder, and salt. Brush this glaze evenly over the entire duck skin. Allow it to dry for another 1-2 hours, then apply a second coat if desired for a richer color and flavor.

instructions.title: Initial Roasting Phase
instructions.text: Preheat your oven to 375°F (190°C). Place the duck on a rack in a roasting pan, breast-side up. Fill the bottom of the roasting pan with about 1 inch of water to create steam and catch drippings. Roast for 30 minutes.

instructions.title: Continue Roasting to Perfection
instructions.text: After 30 minutes, reduce the oven temperature to 300°F (150°C). Continue roasting for another 1.5 to 2 hours, or until the internal temperature of the thickest part of the thigh reaches 165°F (74°C) and the skin is deeply golden brown and incredibly crispy. Baste occasionally with rendered fat if desired.

instructions.title: Rest, Carve, and Serve
instructions.text: Once roasted, remove the duck from the oven and let it rest for 10-15 minutes before carving. This allows the juices to redistribute, ensuring tender meat. Carve the crispy skin and meat into thin, bite-sized slices. Serve the Peking Duck immediately with warm thin pancakes or steamed buns, hoisin sauce, julienned scallions, and fresh cucumber sticks for guests to assemble their own delicious wraps.

tips: For the crispiest skin, ensuring the duck is completely dry before roasting is paramount. The air-drying step is non-negotiable for authentic results.
Maltose gives the authentic sheen and crispness; if using honey, dilute it slightly more with water to achieve a similar consistency.
Serve immediately after carving to enjoy the skin at its peak crispiness. Reheating will diminish the skin's texture.
article: Peking Duck is a beloved Chinese Dinner Recipes dish enjoyed across Chinese. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
